title: Dev-to-Test Handoff
description: Developer agent output consumed by the test agent. The coverage_gaps and properties_believed fields are the core innovation — they tell the test agent exactly where to focus instead of re-discovering everything from scratch.

handoff_type.description: Handoff type identifier. Always 'dev_to_test' for this schema.

files_changed.description: All files created, modified, or deleted during implementation.

path.description: Relative file path from project root.

change_type.description: Whether the file was created, modified, or deleted.

summary.description: One-line summary of what changed in this file and why.

functions_added.description: New functions or methods introduced. Helps the test agent understand the API surface to test.

file.description: File where the function is defined.

signature.description: Full function signature including parameters and return type.

purpose.description: What this function does and when it should be called.

unit_tests_written.description: Summary of unit tests the developer wrote alongside the implementation.

count.description: Total number of unit tests written.

summary.description: Brief description of what the tests cover.

coverage_gaps.description: Things the developer KNOWS are untested or under-tested. Must contain at least 3 items — there are always gaps in error handling, concurrency, and edge inputs. This is the highest-value field in the entire protocol — it tells the test agent exactly where to focus. Be specific: 'race condition when concurrent token refresh' not 'needs more testing'.

properties_believed.description: Properties the developer believes to be true about the implementation, with confidence levels and evidence. The test agent will attempt to FALSIFY medium/low confidence properties.

property.description: A specific, testable property of the implementation. Bad: 'code works correctly'. Good: 'validateToken always returns boolean, never throws'.

confidence.description: How confident the developer is. high: type-checked + tested. medium: tested but edge cases unclear. low: believed true but not verified.

evidence.description: Why the developer believes this property holds. Reference specific tests, type hints, or reasoning.

known_risks.description: Edge cases, race conditions, performance concerns, or security considerations identified but not fully addressed during implementation.

integration_points.description: External systems, APIs, databases, or services this code interacts with. Helps the test agent understand what needs mocking vs integration testing.

inline_requirements.description: Brief requirements extracted from the task description. Used in quick mode when no BA handoff exists. Each item is one requirement or constraint inferred from the task description.

tokens_used.description: Total tokens consumed by the developer agent during this stage.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this handoff was produced.
